Definition 4.6.4.

Suppose that \(D\subset \bbR^{n}\) is open and \(k\in \bbN\text{.}\) We define \(C^{k}(D)\) to be the space of functions which have \(j\) th order continuous partial derivatives in \(D\) for \(1\le j\le k\text{.}\) When \(k=1\) we say functions in \(C^{1}(D)\) are continuously differentiable in \(D\text{,}\) and when \(k\gt 1\text{,}\) we say that functions in \(C^{k}(D)\) are \(k\)-times continuously differentiable in \(D\text{.}\) We define \(C^{k}(\bar D)\) to be the space of functions in \(C^{k}(D)\) such that each of its \(j\) th order partial derivative has a continuous extension to \(\bar D\text{.}\)
in-context
